parsed.summary: Cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff spans 84 files (+6623/-480). The most defensible risk is a test-coverage gap on the critical API-client layer: glm-client.mjs absorbed 155 lines of changes (retry/backoff integration, BigModel error dispatch) but has no dedicated test file, while less critical modules (args, preset-config, fs) each received one. The deleted process.mjs (67 lines) raises a secondary import-graph concern that cannot be fully verified from metadata alone. Review was constrained by absence of file contents for line-level inspection.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs significantly refactored without dedicated test coverage
findings.body: The API client module shows 155 lines of changes across v0.4.x, integrating the new retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) modules. Both of those new modules have dedicated test files (352 and 189 lines respectively), but the integration point — glm-client.mjs itself — does not. The client handles authentication headers, request construction, error classification, and retry orchestration. A silent regression in any of these paths would manifest as failed API calls, dropped jobs, or misclassified errors in production. Less critical modules (args.mjs, preset-config.mjs, fs.mjs) each received dedicated test suites, making this gap inconsistent with the project's own testing standard.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering: (1) retry delegation on transient BigModel errors, (2) error classification flow through bigmodel-errors.mjs, (3) auth header construction, (4) timeout handling, and (5) the integration path where retry + error dispatch interact. If coverage exists indirectly in other test files, document that mapping explicitly so regressions are traceable.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted — residual import references not verifiable from context
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was deleted entirely in the cumulative diff. The commit 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge' suggests dangling references were addressed, but the fix and deletion land in the same cumulative change set. If any dynamic import, indirect require, or string-concatenated path still references 'lib/process', it will surface only as a runtime ReferenceError when that specific code path executes — likely in an edge case or error path during a real sweep run.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs\|lib/process' scripts/` (or rely on check-imports.mjs) to confirm zero remaining references. Pay special attention to dynamic imports, template-literal paths, and test files that may import the deleted module for mocking.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs and glm-companion.mjs changed significantly without dedicated tests
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) manages job state tracking and glm-companion.mjs (139 lines changed) is the main CLI entry point. Neither has a dedicated test file. While job-render.test.mjs (144 lines) may exercise tracked-jobs indirectly, the companion's CLI argument parsing and command dispatch paths lack visible coverage. A regression in command routing would only appear at runtime during an actual invocation.
findings.recommendation: Confirm whether job-render.test.mjs exercises tracked-jobs.mjs paths. Consider at minimum a smoke test for glm-companion.mjs command dispatch to catch argument-parsing regressions before they reach users.

next_steps: Provide full file contents for scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs, scripts/lib/retry.mjs, and scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs for line-level adversarial review
Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs\|lib/process' scripts/ tests/` to confirm no dangling references to the deleted module
Map which test files exercise glm-client.mjs integration paths; if none exist, block until tests/glm-client.test.mjs is added
Verify tracked-jobs.mjs coverage through job-render.test.mjs and document the coverage mapping
